news.title: To generate revenue, Ludhiana civic body eyes sale of commercial properties
news.para: Facing financial strain, Ludhiana's civic body is set to auction its commercial properties, aiming to raise ₹50 crore in the fiscal year 2026-27.  Sixty-five shop-cum-offices in Shaheed Bhagat Singh Nagar and other prime locations are slated for sale. Officials are benchmarking rates against other development authorities to maximize revenue from these valuable assets.

news.title: Indiramma housing phase-II soon, bid to fix issues: Telangana minister
news.para: Telangana's Revenue and Housing Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy announced the imminent launch of the second phase of the Indiramma housing scheme. He highlighted the government's efforts to resolve land record issues and criticized the previous BRS government for failing to fulfill its housing promises. Reddy also reiterated the Congress government's commitment to farmers' welfare, citing crop procurement and loan waivers.

news.title: BMC initiates integration of 3D digital model of Mumbai with civic systems
news.para: The BMC is integrating its 3D digital model of Mumbai with key civic systems to enhance transparency and efficiency. This digital twin will aid in precise assessment of development charges and property tax, detect unauthorized constructions, and streamline approval processes. The system will also support infrastructure planning and disaster management by providing detailed urban landscape data.

news.title: Kolkata: Salt Lake residents get property tax bills
news.para: Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation is now sending property tax bills for the 2026-27 financial year. Residents can get a 5% rebate by paying on time. Late payments will incur a 10% penalty and 10% interest on arrears. Taxpayers can pay online or at designated bank branches. The corporation aims to collect Rs 44 crore this financial year.

news.title: Saint-Gobain to invest $1 billion in India, bets on growth shift
news.para: French company Saint-Gobain will invest nearly one billion dollars in India over the next five years. This investment signals a major shift towards emerging markets for growth. India's growing population and urbanisation are key drivers. Saint-Gobain aims to triple its business in India within ten years. The company is expanding its presence in various infrastructure projects across the country.

news.title: Bengaluru development body not a ‘promoter’: Petition in Karnataka HC
news.para: The Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) has filed a writ petition in the Karnataka high court seeking clarity on the applicability of the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016, to its residential layouts. BDA contends it is not a "promoter" under the Act and argues against retrospective application to projects notified before May 1, 2017.

news.title: Plans to amend building bylaws in Bengaluru met with pushback
news.para: Bengaluru's proposed building bye-law amendments allowing regularization of construction deviations via fees are facing strong opposition. Resident groups argue this legalizes unauthorized building, incentivizes violations, and will strain the city's already burdened infrastructure. Concerns are also raised about the Commissioner's discretionary powers and a lack of transparent consultation.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh's ULBs see 24% rise in property tax collections at ₹1,010 crore in April 2026
news.para: Andhra Pradesh's urban local bodies amassed over Rs 1,010 crore in property tax during April, showcasing a substantial 24.1% increase from the previous year. This leap in revenue can be credited to a comprehensive revenue strategy that incorporates advanced GIS property mapping, drone surveys, and user-friendly digital dashboards.

news.title: Upper-floor eligibility in Dharavi rehabilitation tied to ground-floor status, documents
news.para: For upper-floor dwellers in Dharavi, a new opportunity arises with the government's latest resolution: the chance to secure a 300 sq ft home. However, eligibility hinges on the agreement of those residing on the ground floor. This inclusive initiative offers flexibility, allowing residents to choose between hire-purchase or outright ownership.

news.title: Brick kiln workers in Uttar Pradesh grapple with poor wages, harsh work conditions
news.para: Thousands of migrant workers in Uttar Pradesh's brick kilns face exploitation, lacking fair wages and basic amenities. Despite efforts by collectives like Buniyaad, outdated policies and registration hurdles prevent access to essential government schemes and healthcare, leaving workers in a precarious state.

news.title: Visakhapatnam civic body collects ₹230 crore property tax in April 2026
news.para: Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation (GVMC) witnessed a significant 50% surge in property and vacant land tax collections for April, reaching Rs 230 crore. This increase was driven by a 5% early bird rebate and a government waiver on interest for outstanding dues paid by April 30, encouraging timely payments.

news.title: Hostels & PGs in Tamil Nadu to increase rent by 10% from May 5, as commercial LPG cylinder prices rise
news.para: Hostel and PG rents in Tamil Nadu are set to increase by approximately 10% starting May 5. Operators cite a significant surge in commercial LPG cylinder prices, forcing them to adjust tariffs. Some establishments have also reduced food services or increased maintenance fees due to rising operational costs.

news.title: Cement companies in Meghalaya imported nearly three lakh MT of coal without valid papers: HC-appointed panel
news.para: A high court committee report reveals two Meghalaya cement companies violated norms transporting over 2.93 lakh metric tonnes of coal. The companies moved the fuel without mandatory approvals and failed to file returns. Crucial documents were also missing. The committee recommended strict enforcement and improved tracking systems to curb illegal coal movement across the state.

news.title: Government buildings in Bengaluru face crackdown over ₹473 crore tax arrears
news.para: Bengaluru's revenue wing has directed five city corporations to identify, verify, and recover pending property tax and service charges from government buildings and public sector units. This action follows a complaint highlighting significant arrears, urging authorities to collect dues from government properties across the city.

news.title: DDA extends payment deadline for Towering Heights allottees till June 30
news.para: Delhi Development Authority has extended the payment deadline for Towering Heights flats to June 30, 2026, for both e-auction and FCFS schemes. This decision, approved by the vice-chairman, considers delays in securing statutory approvals and representations from allottees.

news.title: Land pooling policy nod unlocks Haryana’s mega urban expansion plan
news.para: Haryana government has approved significant changes to its land pooling policy. This move paves the way for extensive urban development across 1.67 lakh acres. The state plans to develop new residential, institutional, industrial, and commercial hubs. The first phase is already underway, with acquisition of 40,000 acres in 13 districts. This initiative aims to boost employment and investment opportunities.

news.title: Chhattisgarh government puts illegal sand mining's accountability on collectors
news.para: Chhattisgarh's district collectors are now personally accountable for any illegal sand mining, transportation, or storage within their jurisdictions. The state government is enforcing a zero-tolerance policy, with drone surveys and flying squad inspections to detect violations. Collectors must ensure adequate sand supply at reasonable rates and prevent illegal mining under the guise of royalty-free schemes.

news.title: Mortgage approvals in UK rise to over 63,500: Bank of England data
news.para: British lenders approved the most mortgages in four months in March, with consumer lending growing at its fastest annual rate in over two years. Despite a drop in consumer confidence and concerns over rising mortgage rates, house prices continued to rise modestly in April. Economists anticipate potential interest rate hikes, which could impact future house price inflation.
